title: arctium lappa var. minus
intro: Arctium lappa var. minus, also known as the lesser burdock, is a variety of the burdock plant that is native to Europe and Asia, and is characterized by its smaller leaves and stems compared to the common burdock. The roots and leaves of the lesser burdock plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful for treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of lesser burdock include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, leading to potential health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being. Lesser burdock is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used in cooking and as a natural remedy for skin and hair care.
